witnesses.description: A proper 3-coloring of the triangle K3.
An invalid coloring that assigns the same color to both endpoints of one edge.

checks.claim: There exists a proper 3-coloring of the triangle K3.
checks.notes: The validator checks that every edge has endpoints with different listed colors.

checks.claim: The listed same-color assignment is a proper coloring of a one-edge graph.
checks.notes: The validator confirms the assignment violates the only edge constraint.

checks.claim: There exists a proper 2-coloring of the triangle K3.
checks.notes: The validator enumerates all eight assignments and confirms each has a monochromatic edge; the CNF proof regression parses the DIMACS artifact, emits DRAT, elaborates LRAT, and checks both proofs.

checks.claim: There exists a proper 2-coloring of the triangle K3, encoded as one 1-bit BV color per vertex.

data.certificate: export_qf_bv_unsat_proof emits a DRAT certificate for the bit-blasted CNF; UnsatProof::recheck validates the CNF refutation while graph-to-BV lowering and bit-blast/Tseitin remain explicit trust steps until Lean reconstruction covers the original formula.

checks.notes: The SMT-LIB artifact declares one 1-bit BV color for each triangle vertex and asserts pairwise disequality along all three edges. The generated DIMACS/DRAT pair is rechecked by the regression.
